title: crataegus oxyacantha proles macrocarpa
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha proles macrocarpa, a subspecies of the common hawthorn tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its cardiovascular and digestive benefits, due to its rich content of flavonoids, such as quercetin and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Crataegus oxyacantha proles macrocarpa include reducing blood pressure, improving blood lipid profiles, and providing relief from symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, can be used to support cardiovascular health and overall well-being, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies.
